title: The Abomination of Desolation
subtitle: The coming of Antichrist, and the flight to the mountains of virtue

stillness: Be still, and consider what must come.
Before the glory of Christ, the testing of Antichrist.
Let your heart be quieted, that it may not become foolish.
gospel: When therefore you shall see the abomination of desolation,
which was foretold by Daniel the prophet,
standing in the holy place — let him who reads understand.
Then let those who are in Judea flee to the mountains;
and he who is on the housetop, let him not come down
to take anything out of his house;
and he who is in the field, let him not return
If any man shall say to you, Lo, here is Christ —
behold: As peace went before Christ, so discord goes before Antichrist.
As smoke precedes the fire, and battle the victory,
so before the glory of Christ comes the temptation of Antichrist.
See him called the abomination, by reason of his pride,
by which he sets himself level with God,
claiming for himself the honor that is God's alone.
for then will be the greatest desolation of the elect,
when the worship of God is destroyed.
In the holy place Antichrist shall stand:
where God was once invoked by the prayers of the saints,
he shall seem worthy of the honor due to God.
Already his likeness goes before him,
for already the mystery of iniquity works in his members.
consider: Flee to the mountains — that is, to hidden and deserted places, where you may lie concealed.
He who is on the housetop, in the height of perfection, let him not come down to take anything from his house through greed for worldly things;
for by these even the perfect are often cast down from the summit of perfection in the time of persecution.
He who is in the field, laboring for the fruit of good works, let him not turn back to take up his tunic —
that is, let him not return to the occupations of the world, which multiply sin.
Because of so great a press of tribulations and evils, and the awaiting of the Judge to come,
no one will have leisure to attend to temporal affairs.
Better that a man save his own life than his possessions, and consider how he shall meet the gaze of the Judge.
Those in Judea are the beginners, who stand only in the confession of faith.
Those on the housetop are those advancing, who have climbed above the flesh, above the world, above the air of the demons.
Those in the field are the perfect, who, perfect in themselves, now strive to perfect others.
Let the beginners flee to the eternal mountains — to the Scriptures of the Apostles and Prophets, and to the heights of the virtues, that they may be illumined by God.
Let those who have risen above flesh and world not bend back to carnal desires.
Let him who labors in the Church, like the ox in the Lord's fields, strip himself of every temporal thing,
that, naked, he may follow the naked Christ.
And well is it said that one must not turn back.
Remember Lot's wife: fleeing Sodom, through her intemperance she looked back upon it, and was made a statue of salt.
She signifies those who in tribulation look back, and turn themselves away from the hope of the divine promise.
She was made a pillar of salt, that by her example men might be warned and seasoned, lest they too become foolish.
Abraham looked on, approving God's sentence; she looked back, taking delight in their sins.
Lest men in persecution look back — denying the faith, deserting the good life, to save their bodily life —
he warns: whoever says, Lo, here is Christ, do not believe such men.
prayer: Lord Jesus Christ, in your mercy,
grant me, when I have seen the abominations of the world and the raging tribulations,
to flee to the mountains and the heights of the virtues;
not to come down from spiritual loftiness to carnal desires and the acts of my former worldly life,
nor to bend myself back to them.
Stretching forward to what lies ahead, and laboring for the fruit of good works,
let me not look back to the things behind, to the temporal things of which I have stripped myself.
Grant me also, believing no seducers,
to seek the kingdom of grace, which can be within us,
and so to come to the kingdom of glory. Amen.

latinJewel.english: That, naked, he may follow the naked Christ.
